jokes.text: That's something my ex-wife used to tell me.
jokes.setup: Larry misses the golf hole, someone says 'You're supposed to put it in the hole'
jokes.punchline: Larry connects it to his sexual performance with his ex-wife
jokes.explanation: Classic golf-sex double entendre. Well-timed and economical, fits Larry's character but fairly predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That's something my ex-wife used to tell me.

jokes.text: Artie go out to lunch? He never goes out to lunch.
jokes.setup: Beverly mentions Artie went to lunch
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredulous reaction reveals this is completely out of character
jokes.explanation: Establishes character traits through behavioral anomaly - perfect Larry Sanders writing that sets up the episode's main plot.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Artie go out to lunch?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: He never goes out to lunch.

jokes.text: When I get stressed out, I retain water. Every year before the emmys, I puff up like a fucking blowfish.
jokes.setup: Beverly explains why Larry needs to make a decision about Clint Black
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely specific imagery about stress-induced bloating
jokes.explanation: Perfect Beverly overshare - specific, visual, inappropriate. The blowfish comparison is genuinely funny imagery.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: When I get stressed out, I retain water. Every year before the emmys, I puff up like a fucking blowfish.

jokes.text: Beverly's frantic fan-girl meltdown over Clint Black
jokes.setup: Beverly learns Clint Black is here
jokes.punchline: Complete professional breakdown - hyperventilating, appearance panic, declaring he's her 'whole life'
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe comedy - believable character meltdown that's uncomfortable but funny.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Beverly's frantic fan-girl meltdown over Clint Black

jokes.text: I have to give you a check for the coffee table and the lamp. / No, sir. I intend to make you work for what you owe.
jokes.setup: Post-coital pillow talk establishing they've been intimate
jokes.punchline: Artie's debt becomes sexual payment plan
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning - financial debt becomes sexual debt. Economical and character-specific to Artie's style.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I have to give you a check for the coffee table and the lamp.

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: No, sir. I intend to make you work for what you owe.

jokes.text: What are you doing? Now we don't have a lookout. Now, if he comes in, he's gonna see... his calendar.
jokes.setup: Hank abandons his lookout position during their 'operation'
jokes.punchline: Larry's dramatic concern deflates into the mundane reality - they're just reading a calendar
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation joke - builds tension around covert operation then reveals the anticlimatic reality. Classic Larry Sanders structure.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What are you doing? Now we don't have a lookout. Now, if he comes in, he's gonna see... his calendar.

jokes.text: Where is Artie?! Would you keep it down? You have to speak up, Larry! I can't hear ya!
jokes.setup: Hank is temporarily deaf from air travel
jokes.punchline: The contradiction of Larry asking Hank to keep it down when Hank can't hear
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup that creates escalating miscommunication. The hearing loss provides ongoing comedic conflict.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Where is Artie?! Would you keep it down?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: You have to speak up, Larry! I can't hear ya!

jokes.text: First, she steals Snyder. Now she's trying to steal Artie. Fuckers!
jokes.setup: Larry realizing Donna Lasner is from CBS
jokes.punchline: His paranoid rage about industry poaching
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Larry's industry paranoia and possessiveness

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: First, she steals Snyder. Now she's trying to steal Artie. Fuckers!

jokes.text: August, I think!
jokes.setup: Larry tells Hank to keep the information secret
jokes.punchline: Hank's completely random response showing he understood nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect non-sequitur capper to the hearing loss sequence. Random but feels character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: August, I think!

jokes.text: Darlene volunteering to sing with Clint Black
jokes.setup: Sound check is happening
jokes.punchline: Staff member inserting herself into the show
jokes.explanation: Setup for later cringe - establishes the inappropriate boundary crossing that will escalate.

dialogue.speaker: Darlene
dialogue.line: Darlene volunteering to sing with Clint Black

jokes.text: It's great to find some places never change... except for John, the chef. He's dead.
jokes.setup: Artie waxing nostalgic about Dan Tana's restaurant
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of death deflating the sentiment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie line - dark humor delivered matter-of-factly. Economy of language with maximum impact.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: It's great to find some places never change... except for John, the chef. He's dead.

jokes.text: We split it. / Yeah, well, good. Fascinating. / So we're screwing each other silly. Is that what you want to know about?
jokes.setup: Larry fishing for information about Artie's lunch
jokes.punchline: Artie cuts through the passive-aggression with crude directness
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Artie's bluntness vs Larry's neurotic probing. Realistic escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: We split it.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, good. Fascinating.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: So we're screwing each other silly. Is that what you want to know about?

jokes.text: Ever since I pissed in Larry Tisch's pool on labor day, I've been persona non grata at black rock.
jokes.setup: Artie explaining why he can't work for CBS
jokes.punchline: Specific, outrageous past transgression against CBS executive
jokes.explanation: Legendary Artie line - specific industry reference with perfectly inappropriate behavior. Could only come from this character.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Ever since I pissed in Larry Tisch's pool on labor day, I've been persona non grata at black rock.

jokes.text: You cbs whore.
jokes.setup: Larry being fake-nice to Donna while she steals his producer
jokes.punchline: Immediate behind-her-back insult after pleasantries
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry two-face moment - polite to face, vicious behind back. Economical and character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You cbs whore.

jokes.text: Well, no, not if you're riding fence.
jokes.setup: Darlene asks if her cowgirl outfit is the problem
jokes.punchline: Larry's polite but cutting assessment of her inappropriate costume choice
jokes.explanation: Subtle burn delivered with false politeness - Larry being diplomatically cruel about her delusions.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, no, not if you're riding fence.

jokes.text: They'll cut you like a ripe tomato, OK?
jokes.setup: Hank warning Kris Kristofferson about the show
jokes.punchline: Mangled metaphor combining 'cut you down' with food imagery
jokes.explanation: Classic Hank malapropism - trying to sound tough but bungling the metaphor. Very character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: They'll cut you like a ripe tomato, OK?

jokes.text: Listen, no matter what they tell you, no matter what Larry tells you, they'll cut you like a ripe tomato, OK?
jokes.setup: Crew member warning Kris Kristofferson about the show
jokes.punchline: Vivid metaphor for how the show treats guests
jokes.explanation: Good insider cynicism with colorful metaphor, though not character-specific

dialogue.line: Listen, no matter what they tell you, no matter what Larry tells you, they'll cut you like a ripe tomato, OK?

jokes.text: You must be one hell of a lay.
jokes.setup: Artie mentions Donna offered him Tom Snyder's job
jokes.punchline: Larry's crude reduction of professional success to sexual prowess
jokes.explanation: Crude but character-appropriate jealousy. Not particularly clever but fits Larry's insecurity.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You must be one hell of a lay.

jokes.text: I'm also a good cook, and I mix a stiff drink. Every last one of my wives said they would have divorced me years before, but they just couldn't face life without my herbs and spices.
jokes.setup: Larry's crude comment about sexual prowess
jokes.punchline: Artie's matter-of-fact assessment of what keeps women around - domestic skills, not sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie response - deflates sexual boasting with practical wisdom. Character-specific and well-crafted.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I'm also a good cook, and I mix a stiff drink. Every last one of my wives said they would have divorced me years before, but they just couldn't face life without my herbs and spices.

jokes.text: I'm lying for love.
jokes.setup: Larry accusing Artie of lying about considering the CBS job
jokes.punchline: Artie's romantic justification for deception
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment - Artie's vulnerability disguised as tough-guy behavior. Economical and touching.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I'm lying for love.

jokes.text: You didn't just say tough titty, did you? / Tough titty!
jokes.setup: Larry incredulous at Artie's crude expression
jokes.punchline: Artie defiantly repeating it
jokes.explanation: Good character escalation showing Artie's stubborn crudeness, though the phrase itself is dated

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You didn't just say tough titty, did you?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Tough titty!

jokes.text: Tough titty! / You didn't just say tough titty, did you? / Tough titty!
jokes.setup: Artie getting defensive about Larry's pressure
jokes.punchline: Using deliberately juvenile language as grown man power move
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation - adult using child language defiantly. Larry's disbelief makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Tough titty!

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You didn't just say tough titty, did you?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Tough titty!

jokes.text: I'm afraid I'm going to turn into a lonely old geezer wondering whether your melon got chopped up or whether I told you that your ass isn't fat.
jokes.setup: Larry accusing Artie of being afraid to say no to Donna
jokes.punchline: Artie's genuine fear of aging alone mixed with bizarre concern about Larry's body image
jokes.explanation: Complex character moment - vulnerability mixed with absurd specificity. Classic Larry Sanders emotional breakthrough.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I'm afraid I'm going to turn into a lonely old geezer wondering whether your melon got chopped up or whether I told you that your ass isn't fat.

jokes.text: My ass is fine. My ass is great. I never felt better about my ass.
jokes.setup: Artie's comment about Larry's body image issues
jokes.punchline: Larry's defensive ass-confidence declaration
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of the ass discussion. Perfectly neurotic Larry response with good repetitive structure.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: My ass is fine. My ass is great. I never felt better about my ass.

jokes.text: Then maybe you and your fat ass can go fuck yourselves.
jokes.setup: Larry claiming his ass is fine
jokes.punchline: Artie immediately contradicting with 'fat ass' insult
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalation - undermines Larry's confidence with direct contradiction. Classic Artie meanness.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Then maybe you and your fat ass can go fuck yourselves.

jokes.text: Here's your fucking suit. / Where are the pants? / They're letting them out in the butt.
jokes.setup: Larry needs his suit after the ass argument
jokes.punchline: Immediate confirmation that his butt does need letting out
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - universe immediately contradicts Larry's ass confidence. Well-timed comedic irony.

dialogue.speaker: Wardrobe
dialogue.line: Here's your fucking suit.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Where are the pants?

dialogue.speaker: Wardrobe
dialogue.line: They're letting them out in the butt.

jokes.text: You mean the crotch?
jokes.setup: Being told pants are being let out 'in the butt'
jokes.punchline: Larry's need to be technically correct about tailoring terminology while embarrassed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry neurosis - being pedantic even when humiliated. Character-specific response.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You mean the crotch?

jokes.text: You're the happiest we've ever had on the show.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to compliment Clint Black
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals their usual guests are miserable
jokes.explanation: Accidentally revealing compliment - shows the show's usual atmosphere while trying to be nice.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You're the happiest we've ever had on the show.

jokes.text: You just need to find your Lisa.
jokes.setup: Clint explaining his marital happiness
jokes.punchline: Genuine advice about finding the right partner
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment that sets up the final joke. Not funny itself but necessary setup.

dialogue.speaker: Clint Black
dialogue.line: You just need to find your Lisa.

jokes.text: I think you're my Lisa, and I think I'm yours. / Oh, jeez! What the fuck!
jokes.setup: Clint's advice about finding your 'Lisa' for happiness
jokes.punchline: Larry applying romantic advice to his professional relationship with Artie
jokes.explanation: Perfect series-defining moment - takes romantic advice and applies to dysfunctional work relationship. Classic ending.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I think you're my Lisa, and I think I'm yours.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Oh, jeez! What the fuck!
